_meta.description: A few well-known Tony Hansen / Digital Fire cone-6 base recipes by code, as starting points for formulation. Amounts are batch percentages (may total slightly over 100). Verify live at digitalfire.com/recipe/<code> before relying on them.
_meta.usage: Feed 'materials' into umf.py / cte.py, or scale with convert.py batch. Add colorants/opacifiers via 'additions'.

G2926B.name: G2926B - Cone 6 transparent glossy (whiteware/porcelain)
G2926B.description: Fires high-gloss and ultra-clear with low melt mobility. A reliable, balanced cone-6 transparent base; the standard starting point for cone-6 liner and colored glossy glazes.

G2934.name: G2934 - Cone 6 matte (MgO)
G2934.description: A cone-6 magnesia matte base (silky/dolomite matte family). Matteness comes from MgO, not underfiring. Good for non-functional surfaces; test for cutlery marking/food safety before functional use.
G2934.note: Amounts are a representative version; confirm exact recipe at the source URL.
